name: Skypulse
description:
SkyPulse delivers route-level airline capacity intelligence. Derived directly from U.S. DOT T-100 Segment data for both domestic and international carriers — 190K+ route-month snapshots and 100K+ pre-computed quarter-over-quarter changes — in one MCP toolkit for aviation analysts, airport planners, travel journalists, airline equity analysts, and destination marketing organizations. Point it at a city pair, airport, or market to track capacity shifts, spot new-route launches, identify frequency pullbacks, attribute capacity drivers (frequency vs fleet upgauge), and rank carriers head-to-head.

Features

-Route-level change tracking: route_capacity_change returns growth, decline, launch, and suspension events for any origin–destination city pair over a lookback window, with before/after frequency, inferred seats, aircraft-type mix, and source refs.
-Launch detection: new_route_launches surfaces every carrier × route that began service at a specified airport during a given period, sorted by inferred seat capacity.
-Frequency losers: frequency_losers ranks the steepest service cuts at a market (airport or city), with prior/current frequency and percent change.
-Capacity driver analysis: capacity_driver_analysis decomposes a route's capacity change into its frequency component and its fleet-mix component (upgauge/downgauge), with prior/current aircraft mixes.
-Market leaderboard: carrier_capacity_ranking ranks carriers by total capacity change at a given airport or city, filterable by aircraft category (narrowbody, widebody, regional_jet, turboprop).
Data coverage

-Period: Jul 2025 – Mar 2026 (9 months of T-100 releases, updated as new BTS vintages publish)
-1,780 airports, 293 carriers, 190K+ route-month snapshots
-IATA codes throughout (not raw BTS DOT numerics) — clean B738, A321, CRJ9 etc. ready for downstream reasoning
-Monthly granularity preserved; aggregated to quarterly at read time
-Freshness metadata and single-source confidence notes included on every response
Try asking

-"How has JFK–LAX capacity changed across carriers in the last 12 months?"
-"Which airlines launched new routes out of ORD in Q4 2025?"
-"Show me the biggest frequency cuts at Atlanta in Q4 2025."
-"For United on SFO–ORD, is the capacity change driven by frequency or by fleet swap?"
-"Rank narrowbody carriers by capacity change at DFW in Q4 2025."
-"Which transborder routes to Mexico grew fastest over the last two quarters?"
Agent tips

-Pass origin and destination as 3-letter IATA airport codes (e.g. JFK, LAX); pass market as a 3-letter IATA airport code too (city-level markets are not yet supported).
-period accepts ISO quarters like 2025-Q4; days_back accepts a positive integer (used by route_capacity_change only).
-carrier is a 2- or 3-letter IATA carrier code (e.g. AA, UA, VB); omit it to return all carriers on the route.
-aircraft_category values on carrier_capacity_ranking: narrowbody, widebody, regional_jet, turboprop, or all.
-Every response includes a data_freshness string describing the underlying T-100 vintage and announcement corpus — surface it in answers when users ask "how recent is this data?"
-Every response includes source_refs and a confidence score (0–1); treat anything below 0.6 as single-source evidence.
-Prefer structuredContent when present; it mirrors the JSON payload for schema-aware parsing.

mcpTools.name: route_capacity_change
mcpTools.description: Route-level capacity and frequency change intelligence for a specific airport pair. Returns per-carrier deltas (frequency, inferred seats, aircraft mix) with DOT T-100 provenance and explicit data freshness. Premium answer product unbundled from OAG Schedules Analyser / Cirium Diio Mi for the covered US domestic + US-international market. Good prompts: "Which carriers added the most capacity on JFK-LAX over the past 365 days?" or "Has AA reduced frequency on DFW-ORD this quarter?".

origin.description: IATA origin airport code (3 uppercase letters).

days_back.description: How many days of history to consider. Defaults to 365.

destination.description: IATA destination airport code (3 uppercase letters).

as_of.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the response was computed.

origin.description: Echo of the input origin IATA code.

change_type.description: Classification of the change: launch / suspension / resumption / growth / reduction / gauge_up / gauge_down.

url.description: Optional link to the raw source.

items.description: One provenance reference backing the answer.

known_unknowns.description: Per-row gaps (missing mix data, single-source, etc.) or null.

prior_frequency.description: Total departures in the prior window (null for launches).

current_frequency.description: Total departures in the current window (null for suspensions).

prior_inferred_seats.description: Inferred seat capacity in the prior window.

current_inferred_seats.description: Inferred seat capacity in the current window.

aircraft_type_mix_prior.description: Aircraft type → frequency (departures) mix for the period. Keys are IATA aircraft type codes (e.g. "B738"), values are integer departure counts.

aircraft_type_mix_current.description: Aircraft type → frequency (departures) mix for the period. Keys are IATA aircraft type codes (e.g. "B738"), values are integer departure counts.

changes.description: Per-carrier change objects for the route pair, one row per carrier per comparison window, ordered by most recent comparison.

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence score for the response (0–1).

destination.description: Echo of the input destination IATA code.

url.description: Optional link to the raw source.

items.description: One provenance reference backing the answer.

source_refs.description: Every source that contributed evidence to this response.

data_freshness.description: Explicit freshness label in the form "Source: DOT T-100 <period> (published <month year>) + Press Releases through <month year> — as of <iso timestamp>".

known_unknowns.description: Explicit, human-readable description of any data gaps, lag, or uncertainty. "None identified" if clean.

comparison_period.description: Time windows being compared, e.g. "2025-Q3 vs 2025-Q2". "N/A" when no data was found.

outputSchema.description: Route-level capacity and frequency change intelligence for an origin→destination airport pair.

mcpTools.name: new_route_launches
mcpTools.description: Detect new route launches and service resumptions at a given airport. Returns every launched/resumed carrier-route with inferred seats and effective date. Good prompts: "What new routes launched from ORD in 2025-Q3?" or "Which carriers resumed service to SEA last quarter?".

period.description: Optional period filter, e.g. "2025-Q3" or "2025-08". If omitted, returns all recent periods.

airport.description: IATA airport code to query (matches origin OR destination).

as_of.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the response was computed.

period.description: Echo of the period filter, or "all" if no filter was supplied.

change_type.description: launch = first time observed; resumption = previously suspended service.

url.description: Optional link to the raw source.

items.description: One provenance reference backing the answer.

effective_date.description: ISO 8601 effective date derived from the source window.

comparison_period.description: Comparison window this launch/resumption was detected in.

current_inferred_seats.description: Inferred seats in the current window.

routes.description: One entry per launched or resumed route-carrier combination.

airport.description: Echo of the input airport IATA code.

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence score for the response (0–1).

url.description: Optional link to the raw source.

items.description: One provenance reference backing the answer.

source_refs.description: Every source that contributed evidence to this response.

data_freshness.description: Explicit freshness label in the form "Source: DOT T-100 <period> (published <month year>) + Press Releases through <month year> — as of <iso timestamp>".

known_unknowns.description: Explicit, human-readable description of any data gaps, lag, or uncertainty. "None identified" if clean.

comparison_period.description: Time windows being compared, e.g. "2025-Q3 vs 2025-Q2". "N/A" when no data was found.

outputSchema.description: New route launches and service resumptions at a given airport over the most recent available periods.

mcpTools.name: frequency_losers
mcpTools.description: Ranks routes losing the most frequency, ordered by steepest percentage decline. Optionally scoped to a single market. Useful for competitive intelligence and market contraction analysis. Good prompts: "Which US domestic routes lost the most frequency year-over-year?" or "Which ATL routes are contracting fastest?".

market.description: Optional IATA airport code to scope the leaderboard. If omitted, returns the network-wide leaderboard.

period.description: Optional period filter (e.g. "2025-Q3"). If omitted, returns recent periods.

as_of.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the response was computed.

losers.description: Ordered list of routes with the biggest frequency reductions.

market.description: Echo of the market filter, or null if network-wide.

period.description: Echo of the period filter, or null.

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence score for the response (0–1).

url.description: Optional link to the raw source.

items.description: One provenance reference backing the answer.

source_refs.description: Every source that contributed evidence to this response.

data_freshness.description: Explicit freshness label in the form "Source: DOT T-100 <period> (published <month year>) + Press Releases through <month year> — as of <iso timestamp>".

known_unknowns.description: Explicit, human-readable description of any data gaps, lag, or uncertainty. "None identified" if clean.

comparison_period.description: Time windows being compared, e.g. "2025-Q3 vs 2025-Q2". "N/A" when no data was found.

outputSchema.description: Routes losing the most frequency/capacity, ranked by steepest percentage decline.

mcpTools.name: capacity_driver_analysis
mcpTools.description: Determines whether capacity change on a route is driven by frequency (more/fewer flights) or gauge (larger/smaller aircraft). Returns per-carrier driver classification with aircraft mix evidence. Good prompt: "Did LAX-NRT capacity grow because of more weekly flights or larger aircraft?".

carrier.description: Optional IATA carrier code (2 chars). If omitted, returns all carriers.

as_of.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the response was computed.

carrier.description: Echo of the carrier filter, or null.

driver.description: Primary driver of capacity change. gauge_driven = aircraft up-gauged, frequency_driven = more/fewer flights, mixed = both move together, flat = <5% change, decline = contraction.

aircraft_type_mix_prior.description: Aircraft type → frequency (departures) mix for the period. Keys are IATA aircraft type codes (e.g. "B738"), values are integer departure counts.

aircraft_type_mix_current.description: Aircraft type → frequency (departures) mix for the period. Keys are IATA aircraft type codes (e.g. "B738"), values are integer departure counts.

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence score for the response (0–1).

url.description: Optional link to the raw source.

items.description: One provenance reference backing the answer.

source_refs.description: Every source that contributed evidence to this response.

data_freshness.description: Explicit freshness label in the form "Source: DOT T-100 <period> (published <month year>) + Press Releases through <month year> — as of <iso timestamp>".

known_unknowns.description: Explicit, human-readable description of any data gaps, lag, or uncertainty. "None identified" if clean.

comparison_period.description: Time windows being compared, e.g. "2025-Q3 vs 2025-Q2". "N/A" when no data was found.

outputSchema.description: Determines whether capacity change on a route is frequency-driven (more/fewer flights) or gauge-driven (larger/smaller aircraft).

mcpTools.name: carrier_capacity_ranking
mcpTools.description: Carrier leaderboard by seat capacity change in a given market. Optional aircraft category filter (narrowbody / widebody / regional_jet / turboprop). Good prompt: "Which carriers added the most narrowbody capacity in the Dallas market this quarter?".

market.description: IATA airport code defining the market (matches origin OR destination).

aircraft_category.description: Optional category filter. Only rows whose dominant current aircraft falls in the category are counted.

as_of.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the response was computed.

routes_lost.description: Count of suspension/reduction/gauge_down rows for the carrier in scope.

routes_gained.description: Count of launch/resumption/growth/gauge_up rows for the carrier in scope.

routes_unchanged.description: Count of rows where |freq change| < 5% and |capacity change| < 5%.

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence score for the response (0–1).

url.description: Optional link to the raw source.

items.description: One provenance reference backing the answer.

source_refs.description: Every source that contributed evidence to this response.

data_freshness.description: Explicit freshness label in the form "Source: DOT T-100 <period> (published <month year>) + Press Releases through <month year> — as of <iso timestamp>".

known_unknowns.description: Explicit, human-readable description of any data gaps, lag, or uncertainty. "None identified" if clean.

comparison_period.description: Time windows being compared, e.g. "2025-Q3 vs 2025-Q2". "N/A" when no data was found.

outputSchema.description: Ranks carriers in the given market by total seat capacity change.

parameterCaveats: Optional aircraft category filter (narrowbody / widebody / regional_jet / turboprop)
